step_by_step_reasoning: We first identify the edge devices (BIQSens as ThirdParty, Greengrass, and Lambda on edge) which ingest plant data. These edge components route data to AWS IoT Core. IoT Core splits the data stream: one path goes to Kinesis Streams for real-time analysis (via Kinesis Analytics) and archiving (via S3 for SageMaker model training), and another path goes to IoT Analytics for batch processing and QuickSight dashboards. SageMaker closes the loop by pushing trained models back to the edge Lambda.

graph.name: L&T Technology Services: Edge-to-Cloud IoT Predictive Maintenance
graph.notes: An end-to-end industrial IoT solution that collects sensor data at the edge, performs real-time and batch analytics in the cloud, and utilizes machine learning to redeploy models back to edge devices.

nodes.name: BIQSens
nodes.notes: On-premise sensor and gateway device collecting plant machinery data.

nodes.name: AWS IoT Greengrass
nodes.notes: Manages local compute, messaging, and data caching at the edge.

nodes.name: AWS Lambda (Edge)
nodes.notes: Runs anomaly detection models locally at the edge.

nodes.name: AWS IoT Core
nodes.notes: Ingests data from edge devices and routes it using rules.

nodes.name: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
nodes.notes: Ingests real-time sensor streams for hot-path analytics and cold-path storage.

nodes.name: Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics
nodes.notes: Runs real-time anomaly detection and condition-based monitoring.

nodes.name: Amazon S3
nodes.notes: Acts as a data lake for raw sensor telemetry and model training data.

nodes.name: Amazon SageMaker
nodes.notes: Trains and refines machine learning models using S3 data.

nodes.name: AWS IoT Analytics
nodes.notes: Processes batch data and runs statistical process control.

nodes.name: Amazon QuickSight
nodes.notes: Visualizes batch historical analytics and plant KPIs.

nodes.name: Plant Operator
nodes.notes: Monitors real-time dashboards and receives anomaly alerts.

edges.notes: Sensors report physical machine metrics to Greengrass.
Sensors stream data directly to local Lambda for low-latency processing.
Greengrass routes telemetry to IoT Core in the cloud.
Local Lambda outputs anomalies and status to IoT Core.
IoT Core rules route high-velocity streams to Kinesis.
IoT Core rules send batch data to IoT Analytics.
Pushes real-time alerts and dashboard updates to operators.
Persists raw data into S3 data lake.
Loads accumulated historical datasets for model training.
Deploys newly optimized models back to edge Lambda.
Provides long-term analytical insights directly to users.
